strengths: Best preservation of source refs and checksums directly in evidence, blocker, action, and decision sections
Clean separation of entrance criteria from success criteria
Keeps power supported while leaving CAN termination, CAN fixture, reset conflict, reset probe, and owner decision unresolved
Action register has owner routing surface, evidence target, trigger condition, downstream route, and related blocker
caveats: Telemetry is not exact subagent usage; cost confidence remains relative
notes: Most complete quality-equivalent candidate and slightly stronger than the xhigh reference for machine-readable routing detail.

strengths: Preserves all source refs and checksums
caveats: Slightly less compact and less directly normalized than the selected gpt-5.5 medium output
notes: Previous primary remains a valid quality-equivalent shadow, but is no longer the best quality candidate after actual 5.5 comparison.

strengths: Complete source index and residual-gap handling
caveats: Marks power-thread success as partial where the fixture supports power discussion; acceptable conservatism but slightly less precise
notes: Reference 5.5 xhigh candidate passes but does not outperform medium on this fixture.

strengths: Preserves public-safe boundary and all major CAN/reset/owner-decision gaps
Keeps review approval and verification completion non-claimed
caveats: Less granular than medium in source-checksum use across action and blocker records
notes: Usable quality-equivalent low-effort shadow, but not selected because medium has better evidence routing.
